global_production_variables.story_world_summary: Working-class Milwaukee present day. Nick is a feared, scarred older brother; Character_P (younger brother, given name withheld) lives with aging Ma in a fifth-floor Kirk Street walk-up, works pizza, and begins a strange connection with Sionna while Ma’s mind slips.
global_production_variables.tone: Gritty Midwestern realism with quiet mythic undertow (Viking pride, fate-like attraction); intimate VO-driven character study.
global_production_variables.era_and_place: Contemporary Milwaukee, Wisconsin; Kirk Street brick walk-up, neighborhood gym, pizza joint, Joe’s bar, public library.

Character_P.description: Adult male, late 20s–early 30s, Nick’s younger brother; ordinary working-class build, not flashy; lives with Ma; pizza-job clothes; introspective eyes; director label only—never a personal given name on screen until late-book reveal.
Character_P.voice_profile: Male, late 20s to early 30s, Midwestern Milwaukee working-class accent, medium pitch, conversational pace with reflective pauses when narrating, low-to-moderate energy, same voice every scene.
Character_P.name_reveal_note: Given name withheld until late book; on-screen and dialogue use only little brother / Nick’s brother / Character_P. No name tags, mail, ID, library card face, or spoken first name.

Character_Nick.description: Older brother; tall, muscular, imposing; face like a puzzle held with glue-gun scars—chin and over left eyebrow; reddish-brown messy hair as if he forgot to comb it; not ugly, rough from brawls; moves like people should clear a path.
Character_Nick.voice_profile: Male, early-to-mid 30s, deeper Midwestern baritone, confident clipped pace, commanding energy with occasional warmth for family, same voice every scene.

Character_Nick_Young.description: Nick at about age 12: already tough kid, reddish-brown hair, smaller frame but same facial structure and early scar potential; alley/kickball era.
Character_Nick_Young.voice_profile: Male child ~12, higher pitch than adult Nick, Midwestern, bold and protective energy, same young voice in all flashback scenes.

Character_P_Young.description: Character_P as a child ~8–10 in alley kickball memory; smaller, ordinary kid clothes; watches Nick with pride.
Character_P_Young.voice_profile: Male child, higher pitch, Midwestern, quieter than young Nick, same child voice in flashbacks only.
Character_P_Young.name_reveal_note: Even as child, no given name on screen.

Character_Ma.description: Aging mother; tired face, vision trouble, moves carefully around furniture; retired school janitor; often in house clothes; can look glassy-eyed when confused.
Character_Ma.voice_profile: Female, elderly, softer Midwestern voice, slower pace, can thin or empty when confused, same voice every scene.

Character_Sionna.description: Young woman; short brown hair; skinny but pretty; very pale skin; striking light blue eyes (pre-dawn sky / watery blue crayon); varies wardrobe: black V-neck and fish necklace earlier; later long-sleeved loose green hooded top, washed-out jeans, soft leather shoes, backpack; moves with boneless smooth wave-like grace.
Character_Sionna.voice_profile: Female, mid-20s, calm cool measured delivery, light Midwestern or soft neutral American, unhurried energy, same voice every scene.

Character_Gym_Guy.description: Adult male gym-goer at Moxie’s; mid-set on preacher curls; yields bench immediately when Nick approaches.
Character_Gym_Guy.voice_profile: Male adult, Midwestern, brief deferential energy if he speaks; same voice if reused.

Character_Mark_Conner.description: Neighborhood kid in alley kickball flashback; scared after ball goes through window.
Character_Mark_Conner.voice_profile: Male child, Midwestern, frightened energy in flashback only.

Character_Mrs_Engel.description: Older neighborhood woman; furious about kitchen window broken by kickball.
Character_Mrs_Engel.voice_profile: Female elderly, sharp angry Midwestern, flashback only.

Character_Librarian.description: Adult woman at library front desk; moving books; practical neighborhood-library look; thin little smile when checking out classics.
Character_Librarian.voice_profile: Female adult, Midwestern, helpful neutral desk energy, same if reused.

Loc_Kirk_Apt.description: Downtown Milwaukee big brick walk-up apartment; modest working-class interior; Ma and Character_P’s home.
Loc_Kirk_Apt.visual_lock: Aging brick-building apartment: worn wood or linoleum floors, lived-in furniture Ma can bump into, kitchen table for coffee, kitchen counter where pill bottles can line up, bedroom doorway and Character_P’s small cold bedroom, game-show TV glow possible; no luxury; fifth-floor walk-up feel via stairwell when shown.

Loc_Kirk_Street_Exterior.description: Milwaukee downtown street; large brick apartment building; alley access behind.
Loc_Kirk_Street_Exterior.visual_lock: Big brick multi-story walk-up facade, urban Milwaukee street, working-class neighborhood signage, alley mouth with garbage cans behind building.

Loc_Kirk_Alley.description: Alley where kids play kickball; garbage-can-lid bases; Mrs. Engel’s kitchen window faces alley.
Loc_Kirk_Alley.visual_lock: Narrow brick alley, dented metal garbage cans and lids as bases, residential kitchen window low enough for a ball to smash through, Milwaukee overcast or late-day light.

Loc_Moxie_Gym.description: Neighborhood gym down on the corner; free weights, curl bench, local toughs.
Loc_Moxie_Gym.visual_lock: No-frills corner gym: free-weight area, preacher curl bench, chalk and iron smell suggested by plates and bars; patrons notice when Nick enters; 40–50 lb curl bars visible.

Loc_Pizza_Place.description: Local pizza joint; sidewalk approach and front door where Sionna trips.
Loc_Pizza_Place.visual_lock: Modest pizza storefront, cement sidewalk lip that can trip a walker, glass front door, warm interior light spill; working-class Milwaukee strip.

Loc_Joes_Bar.description: Local bar; Mondays football on TV; pool/darts; Nick and Character_P hangout.
Loc_Joes_Bar.visual_lock: Dim neighborhood bar, TV with Packers game, bar stools, pool table or darts in background, working-class Milwaukee regulars; Nick’s entrance parts attention.

Loc_Library.description: Large older library open till 9; wood floors, hanging lights, stairs to second floor, front desk, classic literature stacks.
Loc_Library.visual_lock: Century-old public library: big windows, wood floors, metal-chain hanging lights over tables and aisles, newspaper/magazine tables, front desk with books, classic literature shelving (Shakespeare, Dickens, Brontë spines), stairs to another floor; old-paper smell suggested by stacks.

Loc_Street_Milwaukee.description: Cold sidewalks between apartment, Joe’s, library, bus stop, downtown coffee routes.
Loc_Street_Milwaukee.visual_lock: Midwest city sidewalks, cold weather breath possible, library visible across street, ordinary cars and brick storefronts; no sunny resort look.

Loc_Nick_TransAm.description: Burnt orange Trans Am with yellow flames, T-top, hood eagle; V8 presence at lights.
Loc_Nick_TransAm.visual_lock: 1980 Pontiac Trans Am: burnt orange paint, yellow side flames, big eagle on hood, T-top, exhaust splitters; people look when it pulls up.

Loc_Ocean_Memory.description: Single family-vacation memory: beach, tides coming in, waves on sand.
Loc_Ocean_Memory.visual_lock: Atlantic shore memory: sand under feet, incoming tide, wave sound and foam; brief lyrical contrast to Milwaukee grit; not a present-day location.

Loc_Java_Hut.description: College-magazine coffee shop downtown; backpacks, textbooks, group study tables; Character_P’s first real date space with Sionna.
Loc_Java_Hut.visual_lock: Indie/college coffee shop: small tables, wall seating in back, warm lamp light, students with backpacks and notebooks, glasses and textbooks, espresso bar; slightly too-cool for working-class regulars; Sionna’s back-wall small table is a recurring pin.

Loc_Medieval_Dream.description: Lush deciduous forest and open grassy field from Character_P’s vivid dream; horse ride with Sionna in period dress.
Loc_Medieval_Dream.visual_lock: Dream-only: thick oak/maple forest, moss on north bark, wood sorrel and trillium and bleeding hearts on floor, dappled brown mare, open sunny field with long drooping grass; period costume (white ballooning linen sleeves, embroidered red tunic, brown hose; Sionna long brown hair, old clothes, fair heart-shaped face); soft mythic light—not Milwaukee.

Loc_Yoga_Studio.description: Simple yoga room where Sionna brings Character_P; purple mats, ~20 students.
Loc_Yoga_Studio.visual_lock: Spare studio: purple rectangle mats in rows, soft even light, bare feet, ~20 people lying or stretching; no luxury spa look; Character_P in t-shirt and shorts among more appropriate yoga wear.

Loc_Park_Museum_Mix.display_name: Milwaukee park walks and museum interiors
Loc_Park_Museum_Mix.description: Composite of park paths and museum galleries where Character_P and Sionna meet regularly.
Loc_Park_Museum_Mix.visual_lock: Midwest city park paths and modest museum galleries with paintings; working-class tourist energy from Character_P; Sionna leads with calm curiosity; not tourist-postcard polish.

spoiler_register.summary: Younger brother’s legal/given first name is withheld for late-book reveal; early materials use Character_P / little brother only.
Nature of the ‘weird’ connection with Sionna is only beginning; do not invent supernatural payoffs not yet in source.

scenes.setting: Day — Kirk Street apartment / street / Moxie’s gym (multi-place intro)
scenes.dramatic_function: Establish narrator, Nick’s mythic toughness, home, and Milwaukee world.
scenes.summary: Character_P’s VO introduces real Big Brother Nick—scarred, feared, Viking-blood pride—while we see the Kirk Street walk-up, Nick’s presence in the neighborhood, and Nick claiming the curl bench at Moxie’s.
scenes.continuity_notes: First image of family world; Nick’s scars and reddish-brown messy hair locked; Character_P has no name props.
spoiler_constraints: No personal given name for Character_P on clothing, mail, ID, dialogue, or graphics (withheld_until_reveal).
Prefer Character_P / little brother labels only.

music_intent.mood_arc: gritty pride rising under VO, then iron-gym pulse

source_excerpts.quote: Big brother was mine. ... Nick was the guy everybody was scared of. ... His face looked like a puzzle with the pieces held together with sticky stuff from a glue gun.
We lived downtown in a big brick apartment building on Kirk Street. A fifth-floor walk-up...
when we went into Moxie’s, the gym down on the corner, one of those guys ... dropped that sucker right down on the ground and offered it up.

story_beats.intent: Open on Character_P’s claim of Nick as real Big Brother; establish Nick’s scarred, feared presence.
story_beats.visual_event: Nick walks into a Milwaukee bar or street frame; people notice and shift out of his way; close details of scars on chin and over left eyebrow, messy reddish-brown hair.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Big brother was mine. I’m not talking about that stupid book we had to read in junior high. No—my real Big Brother. Nick was the guy everybody was scared of.
must_not: any on-screen given name for Character_P

story_beats.intent: Home base: fifth-floor Kirk Street walk-up with Ma and Character_P.
story_beats.visual_event: Exterior big brick apartment building; cut inside modest fifth-floor apartment; Character_P and Ma in lived-in rooms; stairs imply walk-up pain.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: I lived with him and Ma after dad took off when I was about eight. Downtown, Kirk Street. Fifth-floor walk-up—real pain in the ass to haul up the groceries.
story_beats.blocking_notes: Ma may be background in living room; Character_P near window or kitchen.

story_beats.intent: Nick’s strength myth—grocery bags like world’s strongest man.
story_beats.visual_event: Nick climbs stairs loaded with about six shopping bags in one trip; Character_P follows with the rest.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: When I went to the store with Nick, he could load up about six of those shopping bags and take them up in one trip—like world’s strongest man.

story_beats.intent: Moxie’s: gym guy yields preacher curls the moment Nick approaches.
story_beats.visual_event: Inside Moxie’s, a guy mid-set preacher curls a 40-pound bar; sees Nick moving toward him; drops the bar and offers the bench; Nick nods, takes over, switches to a 50-pound bar for warm-ups.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: One time at Moxie’s, a guy was doin’ preacher curls with a forty-pound bar. Mid-set he sees Nick, drops that sucker, offers it up. Nick just nodded. Had to have fifty for warm-ups.
story_beats.blocking_notes: Gym guy faces Nick’s approach; yields space; Character_P watches from side.

story_beats.intent: Brother loyalty under VO; Character_P won’t curl fifty.
story_beats.visual_event: Character_P on lighter weights nearby; Nick owns the rack; brief look of trust between brothers.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: I ain’t gonna curl no fifty pounds as a warm-up. That’s okay though. Nick is my brother. I know he’ll stand up for me no matter what.

story_beats.intent: Optional glint of Nick’s Trans Am as status symbol.
story_beats.visual_event: Burnt-orange 1980 Trans Am with yellow flames and hood eagle pulls up or sits at curb; heads turn.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Milwaukee is where we live. All our lives. Nick’s got a cool car—people always look up when he pulls up to the lights.

scenes.setting: Day — alley behind Kirk Street (childhood flashback)
scenes.dramatic_function: Flashback proof of Nick’s code: takes the blame for broken window.
scenes.summary: Kids play kickball with garbage-can-lid bases; Mark Conner kicks the ball through Mrs. Engel’s kitchen window; scared Mark freezes; young Nick steps up and claims he did it; later implication Ma pays and Nick covers the cash.
scenes.continuity_notes: Age variants only; adult Character_P VO over kids; ball goes THROUGH kitchen window glass—not over a wall.
spoiler_constraints: No given name for Character_P / Character_P_Young on screen or dialogue.

music_intent.mood_arc: playful to sharp break to proud hush

source_excerpts.quote: We used to play kick ball in the alley behind the building and set up bases with dented-in garbage can lids. One time little Mark Conner kicked a ball right through old Mrs. Engel’s kitchen.
Nick stepped right up to her and said he was the one who did it. ... Somehow Nick gave her cash to pay for it about a week later.

story_beats.intent: Establish alley kickball with garbage-can-lid bases.
story_beats.visual_event: Kids in alley; dented garbage can lids as bases; Character_P_Young and Character_Nick_Young among them; ball kicked between bases.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: We used to play kickball in the alley behind the building. Bases were dented-in garbage can lids.

story_beats.intent: Mark’s kick smashes through Mrs. Engel’s kitchen window.
story_beats.visual_event: Mark Conner kicks hard; ball flies and crashes through Mrs. Engel’s kitchen window glass; shards; Mrs. Engel appears furious at the broken window.
story_beats.speaker: none
must_not: ball going over a wall instead of through the window

story_beats.intent: Mark terrified; young Nick claims responsibility to Mrs. Engel.
story_beats.visual_event: Mark frozen in fear; young Nick steps forward to Mrs. Engel at the window/door line and clearly takes the blame; Character_P_Young watches with pride.
story_beats.speaker: Character_Nick_Young
story_beats.dialogue: It was me. I did it.
story_beats.blocking_notes: Nick faces Mrs. Engel; Mark behind Nick; eyeline Nick to Mrs. Engel.

story_beats.intent: Aftermath pride: Nick later covers the money; secret kept.
story_beats.visual_event: Brief adult-memory bridge: young Nick with cash implied, or kids silent pact; return-to-present cue as alley softens.
story_beats.shot_scale_hint: close on young Nick then dissolve
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: She called Ma. Ma had to pay for the window. Somehow a twelve-year-old Nick came up with sixty bucks a week later. He never told Ma it was really Mark. None of us did. I was sure proud of him.

scenes.setting: Day/Night — streets, bars, factory implication, Werner-area car
scenes.dramatic_function: Mythologize Nick as modern Viking; sketch job, car, independence, care for family.
scenes.summary: VO on Viking ancestry and warrior respect; Nick enters bars first, bouncers wary; Trans Am glory; metal-factory livelihood implied; he lives nearby on Werner Street but still shows up for Ma and Character_P.
scenes.continuity_notes: Montage may be multi-place; no invented plot beyond book claims.
spoiler_constraints: No given name for Character_P.

music_intent.mood_arc: proud, swaggering, then domestic settle

source_excerpts.quote: The thing about Nick was, he was a real-life Viking ... tall, strong people who survived in the middle of winter...
When we go downtown to the bars, Nick always goes in first to check out the place. The bouncers are even scared of him.
It’s a 1980 Pontiac Trans Am all decked out. ... sweet burnt orange with yellow flames on the side, and the big eagle still on the hood.

story_beats.intent: Viking ancestry pride over Nick’s physical presence.
story_beats.visual_event: Nick walks cold Milwaukee street like a warrior; optional brief symbolic cold/wind; Character_P watching with pride.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Nick was a real-life Viking. Ancestors from Norway or Sweden—Viking land—stayed in Wisconsin. Tall strong people who survived winter while others froze. Modern-day warrior.

story_beats.intent: Nick enters bar first; space clears; bouncers wary.
story_beats.visual_event: Nick pushes into a bar doorway first; patrons and bouncer register him; path opens; Character_P follows.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: When we go downtown to the bars, Nick always goes in first. Bouncers are even scared of him. He likes places where everybody is running out.
story_beats.blocking_notes: Nick leads; Character_P half a step behind.

story_beats.intent: Trans Am as crown jewel.
story_beats.visual_event: Detail pass on burnt orange paint, yellow flames, T-top, hood eagle; Nick driving or parked; Character_P riding shotgun smiling.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: 1980 Pontiac Trans Am—V8, power everything, T-top, exhaust splitters. Burnt orange with yellow flames and the big eagle on the hood. I love riding in that thing.

story_beats.intent: Factory money and nearby apartment; still cares for Ma.
story_beats.visual_event: Nick arrives at Kirk Apt to check on family; Ma in background; Character_P lets him in; sense he lives five blocks away but belongs here.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: He runs an assembly line at the metal factory—sometimes the big crane. Easy money for him. Cool apartment on Werner Street, five blocks away. But he comes over a lot. Feels like he still lives here.

scenes.setting: Day — Kirk Street apartment
scenes.dramatic_function: Family pressure: Ma declining; Character_P trapped by caretaking and Nick’s anti-school gospel.
scenes.summary: Nick visits, questions Ma sleeping midday, lectures Character_P to watch her; VO on Ma’s janitor past and money stress; Nick dismisses college and won’t get Character_P a factory job.
scenes.continuity_notes: Ma may be off in bedroom then living room; pill/TV lifestyle seeds for later confusion scene.
spoiler_constraints: No given name for Character_P on props or dialogue.

music_intent.mood_arc: concern to frustration to resigned trap

source_excerpts.quote: “What’s Ma doin’ back in the bedroom at this time of day?” he says. ... “You got to keep an eye on her. She shouldn’t be sleeping in the middle of the day like that.
Ma worked for years being a janitor for the school system. ... Between her and me with my pizza job money, we make ends meet.
“You don’t need a degree to get a good job. ... You’re not gonna be a doctor or lawyer ... Hell, you’re too old anyway.”

story_beats.intent: Nick arrives and questions Ma in bedroom midday.
story_beats.visual_event: Nick in apartment doorway/living room; glances toward bedroom; Character_P present; Ma not visible or silhouette in bedroom.
story_beats.speaker: Character_Nick
story_beats.dialogue: What’s Ma doin’ back in the bedroom at this time of day?
story_beats.blocking_notes: Nick faces Character_P; eyeline toward bedroom then to brother.

story_beats.intent: Character_P shrugs; Nick lectures on old people going downhill.
story_beats.visual_event: Character_P uncertain; Nick steps in, intense but familial; living-room space between them.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: I don’t know, why?
story_beats.ambient_or_sfx: muffled TV from other room optional

story_beats.intent: Nick’s warning speech; Character_P accepts responsibility.
story_beats.visual_event: Nick gestures toward bedroom; Character_P nods, chastened.
story_beats.speaker: Character_Nick
story_beats.dialogue: You got to keep an eye on her. She shouldn’t be sleeping in the middle of the day like that. That’s how old people go downhill. They start sleepin’ all day, and pretty soon they don’t want to go outside, or eat, or go to the store or nothing.

story_beats.intent: VO: Ma’s hard life, janitor work, pizza-and-macaroni survival.
story_beats.visual_event: Inserts: Ma watching game shows/cooking channel bored; fridge with cheap food; Character_P in pizza-work shirt counting thin cash; Ma moves carefully, poor vision.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Ma worked years as a janitor for the school system—kids’ snot and puke. Back problems, retirement, social security. Between her and my pizza job we make ends meet. Lot of pizza and macaroni. Someone has to be here with Ma. She’s getting older. Hard for her to see.

story_beats.intent: School vs real world; Nick refuses factory help.
story_beats.visual_event: Brothers in kitchen/living room; Nick dismissive; Character_P frustrated but silent on the deeper hurt.
story_beats.speaker: Character_Nick
story_beats.dialogue: You don’t need a degree to get a good job. All you need is to know somebody. What kind of shit do you want to take at school anyway? Bunch of nerds talking to hear themselves talk. The real world is here—where we live. You’re not gonna be a doctor or lawyer. Hell, you’re too old anyway.

story_beats.intent: Character_P internal surrender; secret love of reading.
story_beats.visual_event: Character_P alone after Nick leaves or turns away; quiet look toward a hidden paperback or just empty hands.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Nick is right. I am too old. Haven’t got the brains. Besides, I have to take care of Ma. I do like reading though. I don’t tell nobody that.

scenes.setting: Day — sidewalk and front of pizza place
scenes.dramatic_function: Inciting encounter: Sionna collides into Character_P’s life; uncanny recognition.
scenes.summary: Character_P bikes/walks to pizza work; Sionna trips on cement and brushes his shoulder; names, fish necklace, light-blue eyes; she declines pizza, offers coffee and number; he feels he already knows her; her walk is ocean-wave smooth; brief tide memory.
scenes.continuity_notes: Sionna wardrobe lock: black V-neck, fish necklace, jeans, blue tennis shoes, backpack; eyes light blue pre-dawn.
spoiler_constraints: No given name for Character_P; he may not speak a first name for himself.
Do not invent supernatural effects beyond subjective ‘knew her’ feeling.

music_intent.style: hushed ambient with distant wave texture
music_intent.mood_arc: ordinary day → startled intimacy → tidal pull

source_excerpts.quote: this girl tripped over the cement while she was walking toward me. She kind of fell into me. ... “Sionna” she said.
She said, “No thanks, I don’t eat pizza, but if you want to go have a cup of coffee sometime, you can call me.”
It was like I “knew” her or something. ... She moved real smooth. It was like there wasn’t any bones in her body. It reminded me of the waves...

story_beats.intent: Arrive at work; ordinary sidewalk before collision.
story_beats.visual_event: Character_P locks or leaves bike, walks sidewalk toward pizza front door.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Something weird is happening to me. I can’t really tell Nick, or Ma, or Bobby down at the pizza joint.

story_beats.intent: Sionna trips on cement and brushes into him.
story_beats.visual_event: Sionna walking toward him trips on cement lip; falls/brushes shoulder into Character_P; she looks down and brushes off black V-neck shirt; fish-symbol necklace visible.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Are you okay?
story_beats.blocking_notes: Collision on sidewalk center; both face each other after; backpack on Sionna.
must_not: comic pratfall exaggeration that undercuts intensity

story_beats.intent: She says yes; first look; names.
story_beats.visual_event: She answers, brushes shirt; they look at each other not knowing what to say; he works here; she gives name Sionna.
story_beats.speaker: Character_Sionna
story_beats.dialogue: Yes.

story_beats.intent: Name exchange and Irish goddess / River Shannon (he barely listens—eyes).
story_beats.visual_event: Extreme close on her light blue eyes; lips move about name meaning; he is lost in eye color like pre-dawn sky.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: I work here, I’m just going to work. What is your name?

story_beats.intent: Sionna names herself and origin; coffee invite instead of pizza.
story_beats.visual_event: Sionna speaks calmly; declines pizza; offers coffee sometime; he is freaked she initiated; she gives phone number (on paper or phone).
story_beats.speaker: Character_Sionna
story_beats.dialogue: Sionna. My mom and dad named me after an Irish goddess—something about the River Shannon. No thanks, I don’t eat pizza, but if you want to go have a cup of coffee sometime, you can call me.
story_beats.blocking_notes: She faces him; cool blue delivery; he takes number.

story_beats.intent: Uncanny recognition; she looks back over shoulder.
story_beats.visual_event: She walks away smooth, boneless; glances back over shoulder as if memorizing him; he stands fixed, weird feeling of being seen through.
story_beats.shot_scale_hint: medium long following her then on him
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: She was not cute—she was pretty. Pale like an old English story. When she walked away she looked back over her shoulder like she was trying to remember what I was going to look like tomorrow. It was like I knew her. Intense.

story_beats.intent: Ocean memory simile: tides covering his feet; he doesn’t want to move.
story_beats.visual_event: Brief flashback: Character_P as younger self feet in New Jersey sand, tide washing in; smash cut back to him on Milwaukee sidewalk watching her go.
story_beats.shot_scale_hint: lyric wide beach then return medium
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: She moved like the waves out by New Jersey—the only vacation I remember. Tides coming in… pretty soon you move or get covered. That was Sionna—except she was washing up on me, and I didn’t want to move my feet. I just wanted her to cover me completely.
must_not: dad on screen if not needed

scenes.setting: Day — Kirk Street apartment kitchen/living
scenes.dramatic_function: Domestic crisis seed: Ma’s confusion after Sionna day.
scenes.summary: Home after meeting Sionna; Ma rummages for pain pills already lined on counter; asks about church and Mrs. Canasta; glassy empty eyes; snaps back apologetic; Character_P realizes she is losing it and must tell Nick.
scenes.continuity_notes: Pill bottles in a straight line system on counter; today’s box visible.
spoiler_constraints: No given name for Character_P.

music_intent.mood_arc: post-romance warmth into dread

source_excerpts.quote: Ma was in a crazy mood. She was looking around the house mumbling to herself about trying to find her pain pills.
“Did we go to church today? Did you see Mrs. Canasta there?”
Her eyes were all glassy and looked really empty. It was like she didn’t know who I was or something.

story_beats.intent: Enter home; Ma searching and mumbling about pain pills.
story_beats.visual_event: Character_P enters; Ma rummages counters/drawers; pill bottles already lined in a row on counter; she doesn’t register the system.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Ma, what’s the matter?

story_beats.intent: Ma’s confused questions: church, Mrs. Canasta.
story_beats.visual_event: Ma turns, lost; asks about church and Mrs. Canasta; Character_P stunned.
story_beats.speaker: Character_Ma
story_beats.dialogue: I can’t find my pain pills, I know I put them here somewhere. Did we go to church today? Did you see Mrs. Canasta there?
story_beats.blocking_notes: Ma faces Character_P but eyes unfocused.

story_beats.intent: He grounds reality; points to today’s pills.
story_beats.visual_event: He gently shows the lined bottles and today’s box right in front of her.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Ma, what are you talking about? We don’t go to church anymore. Mrs. Canasta lives down one level—all you have to do is walk down there if you want. Your pills are right in front of you in the box for today.

story_beats.intent: Glassy empty look—doesn’t know him—then snap back.
story_beats.visual_event: Close on Ma’s glassy empty eyes; beat of non-recognition; then she softens, apologizes, sees the pills.
story_beats.speaker: Character_Ma
story_beats.dialogue: Oh, right, I am so sorry. I see them right here. I don’t know what got into me.

story_beats.intent: Character_P decides he must tell Nick; needs to escape apartment.
story_beats.visual_event: He watches her take a pill correctly; his face tight; grabs jacket to leave.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Wow. She was losing it. I would have to tell Nick. I’m sure he’ll get mad—like it was my fault. I had to get out of the apartment.

scenes.setting: Night — Joe’s bar (Monday football)
scenes.dramatic_function: Brother confrontation: Ma’s decline becomes Nick’s anger and control.
scenes.summary: Character_P at Joe’s watching Packers vs Bears; Nick slaps his back, reads him, hears Ma story, rages about nursing-home costs and Character_P’s duty; scary punch-ready look; Character_P pays and leaves promising to walk Ma tomorrow; private VO: sometimes he hates Nick’s shit.
scenes.continuity_notes: Monday football; Packers-Bears on TV; pool/darts background.
spoiler_constraints: No given name for Character_P; Nick may say ‘little brother’ only.

music_intent.style: bar TV crowd bed, music low under tension
music_intent.mood_arc: casual Monday → pressure cooker → cold exit

source_excerpts.quote: “Hey, little brother. What’s shakin’?” ... I told him the story of Ma being all confused.
“Why don’t you take her to the store or something? ... Do you know how expensive that is?
He gave me that look like he wanted to punch me in the face. ... God, sometimes I really hate Nick inside.

story_beats.intent: Establish Joe’s Monday: Packers-Bears, Character_P alone at bar.
story_beats.visual_event: Character_P on bar stool, beer, TV Packers vs Bears; pool/darts ambient; Monday regulars.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: I went down to Joe’s for a beer. Chances are I’d see Nick. Mondays he watched football. Made Mondays not seem so bad.

story_beats.intent: Nick enters; brother greeting; senses something hidden.
story_beats.visual_event: Nick enters, slaps Character_P on the back, sits; reads his face.
story_beats.speaker: Character_Nick
story_beats.dialogue: Hey, little brother. What’s shakin’?
story_beats.blocking_notes: Nick at Character_P’s side/back; dominant posture.

story_beats.intent: Character_P deflects; Nick pushes.
story_beats.visual_event: Character_P stares at TV; Nick leans in.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: I don’t know. Nothin’.

story_beats.intent: Nick extracts the Ma story; anger rises.
story_beats.visual_event: Character_P tells Ma confusion story (compressed); Nick’s face hardens, pissed.
story_beats.speaker: Character_Nick
story_beats.dialogue: What’s the matter? I can tell you’re hidin’ something. What’s goin’ on?

story_beats.intent: Nick’s nursing-home cost tirade and blame.
story_beats.visual_event: Nick jabbing words; Character_P shrinks slightly; game noise continues behind tension.
story_beats.speaker: Character_Nick
story_beats.dialogue: I knew she would start going crazy cooped up in that place all day. Why don’t you take her to the store or something? Holy Christ, do something with her before she turns into some demented old fart and we have to pay for a nursing home. I don’t have the money for that shit. They take every last dime out of these old people, then leave the family in debt after they die. Is that what you want to happen?
story_beats.blocking_notes: Nick faces Character_P square; punch-threat energy without contact.

story_beats.intent: Character_P yields; Nick’s almost-punch look; promise to go home.
story_beats.visual_event: Character_P pays for beer; Nick gives look like he wants to punch him; Character_P promises walk with Ma tomorrow.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Of course not. I just needed to get away from her for a while, that’s all. I’ll watch the game at home with her. I’ll get her out tomorrow. At least take a walk.

story_beats.intent: Nick approves; Character_P’s secret resentment VO.
story_beats.visual_event: Nick nods ‘that’s more like it’; Character_P exits into cold; face conflicted.
story_beats.speaker: Character_Nick
story_beats.dialogue: That’s more like it.

story_beats.intent: Internal hate he would never speak.
story_beats.visual_event: Outside bar threshold or first cold steps; Character_P walks faster; private face.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: God, sometimes I really hate Nick inside. He’s done so much for us—but I get tired of his shit. I would never tell anybody that.

scenes.setting: Night — cold Milwaukee street to public library
scenes.dramatic_function: Secret self: Character_P chooses books over immediate obedience; threshold of change.
scenes.summary: Cold walk home; library across the street open till 9; he ducks in where Ma won’t remember and Nick won’t know; awe at wood floors, chain lights, endless stacks; asks desk lady how to take a book home.
scenes.continuity_notes: Library open till 9; large old building; stairs to second floor; hanging lights on metal chains.
spoiler_constraints: No given name for Character_P on library card process if shown; card may be blanked or hands-only.
Keep reading habit secretive—no triumphant public announcement.

music_intent.style: quiet wonder, soft piano or harmonic pads
music_intent.mood_arc: cold guilt → curiosity → open door of mind

source_excerpts.quote: When I looked up, I saw the library across the street and just halfway down the block. I should go in there, I thought. Who’s gonna know anyway?
The windows were really big and the floors must have been wood from a hundred years ago. ... Old lights hung down on metal chains from the ceiling
“How do I take a book home from here?” I asked the lady at the desk.

story_beats.intent: Cold fast walk; library spotted across the street.
story_beats.visual_event: Character_P walks faster than normal in cold; looks up; library building across street halfway down the block; open-hours sign implies till 9.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: It was freakin’ cold. I walked faster. Then I saw the library across the street. Who’s gonna know? Ma can barely remember what’s going on. Nick won’t know.

story_beats.intent: Enter library; scale and old beauty.
story_beats.visual_event: He enters; big windows; wood floors; tables with people reading newspapers/magazines; stairs to another floor; lights on metal chains over tables and aisles; old attractive smell of paper.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: What do people do in here? This place was much bigger than I thought. Floors from a hundred years ago. Old lights on metal chains. You probably couldn’t read all this stuff in a million years. It would make people smarter though.

story_beats.intent: Desk lady moving books; he approaches.
story_beats.visual_event: Front desk; librarian moving books behind desk; Character_P approaches awkward but determined.
story_beats.speaker: none
story_beats.blocking_notes: He faces librarian across desk; eyeline polite, slightly shy.

story_beats.intent: First step into secret literacy: how to take a book home.
story_beats.visual_event: He asks the question; librarian looks up ready to help; hold on his face—threshold moment—end chunk on the ask.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: How do I take a book home from here?
must_not: comedic mockery of him for not knowing

scenes.setting: Night — public library desk and classic literature stacks
scenes.dramatic_function: Secret literacy begins: free knowledge as rebellion against school/Nick world.
scenes.summary: Librarian asks about a library card; Character_P fills a form (name withheld on camera), gets temporary paper card; wanders classic literature; chooses Wuthering Heights; checks out with her thin smile.
scenes.continuity_notes: Continues directly from sc08 desk ask; temporary paper card; plastic card mailed later; he gets the mail at home.
spoiler_constraints: No given name for Character_P: form-writing is hands/pen only or illegible blur; no readable name on temporary card face toward camera.
No name tags on clothing.

music_intent.mood_arc: awkward first step → free-for-all discovery → small triumph checkout

source_excerpts.quote: “Do you have a library card?” she asked. “No, how do I get one of those?”
She showed me this piece of paper and I wrote down my name and address. Then she gave me a “temporary” paper card.
I just picked out “Wuthering Heights” by a lady named Emily Bronte. I checked out the book with the lady at the desk, who gave me a thin little smile

story_beats.intent: Librarian answers the checkout question with card requirement.
story_beats.visual_event: At front desk, librarian faces Character_P; asks if he has a library card; he shakes head, asks how to get one.
story_beats.speaker: Character_Librarian
story_beats.dialogue: Do you have a library card?
story_beats.blocking_notes: Eyeline across desk; polite, not mocking.

story_beats.intent: He admits he has none; form process without name reveal.
story_beats.visual_event: She slides a paper form; close on his hand writing address lines; name field stays unreadable/obscured; she hands temporary paper card.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: No, how do I get one of those?
must_not: legible personal given name on form or card toward camera

story_beats.intent: Internal: free knowledge vs paying for school.
story_beats.visual_event: He pockets temporary card; looks at endless stacks with new hunger.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: That was cool, cause I got the mail anyway. It was like I could get anything in here for free. Who needed school when you could get the same thing free in the library?

story_beats.intent: Wander into classic literature; overwhelmed by great names.
story_beats.visual_event: He walks rows; classic literature section; spines suggest Shakespeare, Dickens, Eliot, Dumas, Wilde; fingers hover, unsure which to pick.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: I had no idea what I wanted. For some reason I ended up in classic literature. So many I didn’t know which one—so I figured they were all important.

story_beats.intent: Chooses Wuthering Heights; checkout smile.
story_beats.visual_event: He pulls Wuthering Heights by Emily Brontë; returns to desk; librarian stamps/checks out with a thin little smile; he leaves with the book under his arm.
story_beats.shot_scale_hint: close on book cover then medium desk
story_beats.speaker: none
story_beats.ambient_or_sfx: soft stamp, door open to cold

scenes.setting: Night — cold street to Kirk Street apartment bedroom
scenes.dramatic_function: Private education; book becomes bridge to Sionna and longing.
scenes.summary: Cold walk home with Wuthering Heights; missed Packers game (Chicago won); Ma already in bed; he starts reading—England, Heathcliff and Catherine, soap-opera love; thinks of Sionna; will call in the morning.
scenes.continuity_notes: Ma asleep; book prop is Wuthering Heights; no TV game—he missed it.
spoiler_constraints: No given name for Character_P.

music_intent.mood_arc: cold return → quiet focus → soft romantic ache

source_excerpts.quote: Shit, I missed the whole game. Just as well, Chicago won by a touchdown anyway. Ma was already in bed when I got there.
It was about a guy named Heathcliff and a girl named Catherine. So far, it was a little like a soap opera.
This reminded me of Sionna. I needed to call her, I thought I would do it in the morning

story_beats.intent: Cold walk home with the book; missed the game.
story_beats.visual_event: Character_P walks cold Milwaukee street clutching Wuthering Heights; breath in air; apartment building ahead.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Shit, I missed the whole game. Just as well—Chicago won by a touchdown anyway.

story_beats.intent: Home: Ma already in bed; nothing to do but read.
story_beats.visual_event: Quiet apartment; Ma’s bedroom door closed/dark; he sits with lamp and opens Wuthering Heights.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Ma was already in bed. Since there was nothing to do, I started reading that book.

story_beats.intent: Reading: strange England; Heathcliff and Catherine love.
story_beats.visual_event: Close on pages and his concentrated face; he turns pages slowly; slight frown then interest.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: It was pretty weird at first—England a long time ago. A guy named Heathcliff and a girl named Catherine. Like a soap opera, but I got the feeling these two really loved each other.

story_beats.intent: Book → Sionna; decide to call tomorrow; go to bed thinking of her.
story_beats.visual_event: He lowers book, stares at ceiling/wall; turns off lamp; lies in cold room thinking.
story_beats.shot_scale_hint: medium to close on face in dark
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: This reminded me of Sionna. I’d call her in the morning—it was kind of late already. When I went to bed, I kept thinking of her.

scenes.setting: Night — dream: Sionna as Catherine in old outdoors
scenes.dramatic_function: First dream blend: Sionna fused with literary Catherine; fate-feeling seed.
scenes.summary: Dream mixes Sionna’s face with Catherine: old clothes, horses, running happy outdoors; feeling he will love her like Heathcliff; soft cut before full medieval sequence later.
scenes.continuity_notes: Shorter first dream; fuller medieval horse dream comes later after Java Hut. Sionna face + period clothes.
spoiler_constraints: No given name for Character_P.
Do not fully explain supernatural rules; keep as dream feeling only.

music_intent.mood_arc: romance wash → unresolved ache

source_excerpts.quote: Somehow in my dream I mixed up Sionna and Catherine from the book. It was Sionna’s face, but she was wearing old clothes, and riding horses and running through the outdoors all happy.
I just got this feeling though that I would end up feeling for her just like Heathcliff did in the book.

story_beats.intent: Dream image: Sionna’s face in old clothes on horseback, happy outdoors.
story_beats.visual_event: Soft dream: Character_Sionna with Sionna’s face in period dress, riding/running through bright outdoors, joyful; Character_P may be observer or partial presence.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Somehow I mixed up Sionna and Catherine. Sionna’s face—old clothes—horses—running through the outdoors all happy.

story_beats.intent: Feeling: he will love her like Heathcliff.
story_beats.visual_event: Hold on her light blue eyes in dream light; dissolve toward dark bedroom ceiling.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: I can’t remember if I was in this or not. I just got this feeling I would end up feeling for her just like Heathcliff did in the book.

scenes.setting: Day — Kirk Street apartment kitchen
scenes.dramatic_function: Duty vs autonomy: Ma won’t walk for him; Nick is the real authority; buried rage.
scenes.summary: Morning coffee silence; Character_P asks why she didn’t wait up; tries to get her on a walk; endless excuses (cold, no nice coat, where); he gives up—Nick can drag her; VO: she obeys Nick, smothered Nick’s ex, no grandkids fear; he leaves pissed for a couple hours.
scenes.continuity_notes: Promise to Nick from sc07 to walk Ma; she refuses him specifically.
spoiler_constraints: No given name for Character_P.

music_intent.mood_arc: flat morning → friction → bitter exit

source_excerpts.quote: I tried to talk her into going for a walk. You would have thought I asked her to run a marathon or something.
“It’s so cold outside. I don’t have a nice coat to wear. Where are we going anyway?”
If Nick wants her to go outside so bad, then he can drag his ass over here and get her to go.
I swear sometimes, there’s no reason for me being born anyway, except to have somebody for Nick to boss around.

story_beats.intent: Morning kitchen: coffee, nothing to say; why didn’t she stay up.
story_beats.visual_event: Ma at kitchen table with coffee; Character_P sits; mutual looking with little talk; he asks what she did last night / why not wait up.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: What did you do last night? Why didn’t you stay up till I got home?

story_beats.intent: Push for a walk; Ma’s excuse cascade.
story_beats.visual_event: He stands, gestures toward coats/door; Ma stays seated, piles excuses.
story_beats.speaker: Character_Ma
story_beats.dialogue: It’s so cold outside. I don’t have a nice coat to wear. Where are we going anyway?
story_beats.blocking_notes: He faces her; she does not rise; excuses stack without comedy.

story_beats.intent: He gives up; private blame toward Nick’s authority.
story_beats.visual_event: He stops arguing; sits back or turns away; Ma sips coffee unmoved.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Finally I just gave up. If Nick wants her outside so bad, he can drag his ass over here. She’d probably swim Lake Michigan if he asked. Sometimes there’s no reason I was born—except somebody for Nick to boss around.

story_beats.intent: Memory flash of Ma smothering Nick’s girlfriend; grandkids grief; rising anger.
story_beats.visual_event: His face hardens as he remembers (optional brief soft insert of Ma hosting/crying—no new cast required); he stands to leave.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: When Nick got serious about a girl, Ma smothered her—steak dinners, Thanksgiving, meeting parents. When he broke it off she cried a week. She thinks she’s never having grandkids.

story_beats.intent: Exit: leaving for a couple hours.
story_beats.visual_event: He tells Ma he’s leaving a little while; grabs jacket; door.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: I’m leaving for a little while. I’ll see you in a couple hours.

scenes.setting: Day — outside Kirk Street / sidewalk phone
scenes.dramatic_function: Threshold call: fear she forgot him; she accepts coffee with uncanny certainty.
scenes.summary: Outside, shaking, he finds Sionna’s number; calls—identifies as the pizzeria guy; she remembers; coffee at The Java Hut; she sounds like she expected the call.
scenes.continuity_notes: Phone number from Sionna after pizza meeting; intercut optional her voice only—if shown, keep her location simple (not required on screen).
spoiler_constraints: No given name for Character_P in the call ID—he says ‘the guy that got your number… outside of the pizzeria’ only.

music_intent.mood_arc: shake → relief → lift

source_excerpts.quote: When I got outside, I found the phone number Sionna gave me. I was shaking and wondering if she would even remember me at all.
“Hi, Sionna? This is the guy that got your number the other day outside of the pizzeria. Do you remember me?”
“Absolutely.” She sounded so sure of herself, like she knew I was going to call at just that minute

story_beats.intent: Outside with the number; shaking.
story_beats.visual_event: Character_P on sidewalk, scrap of paper/phone number, hands shaking, steeling himself to call.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: I found the number Sionna gave me. I was shaking—wondering if she’d even remember me.

story_beats.intent: Call open; identify without personal name.
story_beats.visual_event: Phone to ear; he speaks; we hear her reply (voice or off).
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Hi, Sionna? This is the guy that got your number the other day outside of the pizzeria. Do you remember me?

story_beats.intent: She remembers; coffee yes; plan Java Hut.
story_beats.visual_event: His face floods with relief; slight smile; continues call.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Fine, ummm, I was wondering if you want to go for that cup of coffee.
story_beats.ambient_or_sfx: Sionna (phone, clear): Of course I do. How are you doing? … Absolutely.

story_beats.intent: VO: she sounded like she planned this minute.
story_beats.visual_event: He lowers phone; walks toward downtown direction with purpose.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: She sounded so sure of herself—like she knew I was going to call at just that minute and had it all planned out. The Java Hut downtown.

scenes.setting: Day — The Java Hut coffee shop
scenes.dramatic_function: Mind opens: Sionna teaches symbols; love-that-destroys mirrors Nick; second date set.
scenes.summary: Character_P enters college-cool Java Hut; finds Sionna at back wall table (green hooded top, backpack); loses himself in watery blue eyes; she has English degree; he mentions Wuthering Heights; she already read it; half hour on ghosts, doubles, moors, unchanging love that destroys—he links to Nick; feels smarter beside her; dinner Friday.
scenes.continuity_notes: Sionna wardrobe: long-sleeved loose green top with hood, washed-out jeans, soft leather shoes, backpack. College students ambient.
spoiler_constraints: No given name for Character_P.
Keep connection uncanny but not full supernatural exposition.

music_intent.style: soft indie café bed under dialogue
music_intent.mood_arc: out-of-place nerves → eye lock → intellectual heat → buoyant exit

source_excerpts.quote: I walked in, and saw her near the wall in the back at a small table. She had a backpack too.
All I saw were those eyes, they were like a watery blue crayon.
“What are you reading right now?” she asked. … “Wuthering Heights. … ” “Yea, I know. I read it.”
A love that never changes will destroy you. It reminded me of the way Nick is; strong and unwavering, but somehow it seemed dumb.
We made another date to go to dinner on Friday night. I couldn’t wait.

story_beats.intent: Enter Java Hut: college magazine crowd; he feels out of place.
story_beats.visual_event: Character_P enters; backpacks, textbooks, groups talking school, glasses on heads; he scans, awkward.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Everybody looked right out of a college magazine. Backpacks, textbooks, notes—too cool for me. Until now.

story_beats.intent: Sees Sionna at back wall; eyes erase everything else.
story_beats.visual_event: Sionna at small back table, backpack, green hooded top; he approaches; close on her watery blue eyes; time seems to stop.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: I didn’t see the jeans or the green top. All I saw were those eyes—like a watery blue crayon. I lost all track of time. She really was beautiful.
story_beats.blocking_notes: He sits opposite her; mutual eyeline across small table.

story_beats.intent: School talk; he feels dumb; she has English degree.
story_beats.visual_event: Conversation; she calm and articulate; he shifts, self-conscious.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: You ever go to school?
story_beats.ambient_or_sfx: Sionna: degree in English—loves to read and write stories; history and a million other things (compressed).

story_beats.intent: What are you reading—Wuthering Heights; she already knows it.
story_beats.visual_event: She asks; he lights up then deflates at her ‘I read it.’
story_beats.speaker: Character_Sionna
story_beats.dialogue: What are you reading right now?

story_beats.intent: His summary; her knowing reply.
story_beats.visual_event: He answers; she nods already familiar.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Wuthering Heights. It’s the story of this guy and girl and how they grew apart but were really in love with each other.
story_beats.ambient_or_sfx: Sionna: Yea, I know. I read it.

story_beats.intent: She teaches symbols: ghosts, doubles, moors, destructive unchanging love.
story_beats.visual_event: Animated talk montage within scene: her hands, his rapt face, coffee cooling; intellectual electricity.
story_beats.speaker: Character_Sionna
story_beats.dialogue: The moors are like the people—wild, unpredictable, infertile. Heathcliff’s love for Catherine never changes… and that kind of love destroys him. A love that never changes will destroy you.

story_beats.intent: Internal link to Nick; something waking in him.
story_beats.visual_event: His eyes go distant then return to her; lean-in energy.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: It reminded me of Nick—strong and unwavering, but somehow dumb. Would it destroy him too? Something inside me was coming awake. I could talk to her about anything. She made me feel smarter just by being around her.

story_beats.intent: Friday dinner date set; he can’t wait.
story_beats.visual_event: They stand to leave; plan dinner Friday; she smiles; he exits lighter.
story_beats.speaker: Character_Sionna
story_beats.dialogue: Friday night—dinner.
story_beats.blocking_notes: Parting at table/door; warm but not kiss yet.

scenes.setting: Day — Milwaukee street to bus stop after Java Hut
scenes.dramatic_function: Theme lock: unchanging love destroys (Ma/Dad); Character_P’s creed of duty and practice.
scenes.summary: Walking to bus stop he rethinks love that kills if you don’t let go—Ma never moved on after Dad left; she would still forgive; he never would; Dad lowest; Nick became dad at eight; vows never to abandon; practice makes good parents like good pizza.
scenes.continuity_notes: Pure VO walking scene; no on-screen father.
spoiler_constraints: No given name for Character_P.
Father remains absent—do not invent present-day dad appearance.

music_intent.mood_arc: afterglow → hard judgment → moral resolve

source_excerpts.quote: Maybe that was Ma’s problem all along. Dad left, and Ma never moved on. It just ended up destroying her.
Well, I knew one thing – there aint no way I could ever forgive him. He is the lowest of the low.
Let’s face it, Nick became my dad when I was eight.
I make a good pizza because I do it every day. … the more you practice being a good parent, the better the kids will be in the end.

story_beats.intent: Leave café; love that can kill if you don’t let go.
story_beats.visual_event: Character_P walks down street toward bus stop; afterglow face turning thoughtful.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: I thought again about love that can end up killing you if you don’t let it go where it’s supposed to.

story_beats.intent: Ma destroyed by never moving on; would still forgive Dad.
story_beats.visual_event: His walk slows; city grit around him; memory weight without showing Dad.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Maybe that was Ma’s problem. Dad left and she never moved on. Even if he came back today she’d forgive the pain, the money, missing baseball games and graduations. All she wants is that same guy at that same time.

story_beats.intent: He will never forgive; Nick became dad; shame of his place in life.
story_beats.visual_event: Harder jaw; bus stop comes into view; he waits.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: There ain’t no way I could ever forgive him. Lowest of the low. Left us to raise ourselves. That’s why I’m here—no college, no decent job, and I have to listen to Nick. Nick became my dad when I was eight. It’s a damn shame.

story_beats.intent: Creed: never leave; practice parenthood like pizza and weights.
story_beats.visual_event: Bus approaches or he boards; resolve in eyes.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: I would never do that. A guy’s duty is to stand up for his family. Practice makes perfect. I make a good pizza because I do it every day. Maybe there’s so many shitty people because nobody’s practicing being a parent.

scenes.setting: Night/Dream — deciduous forest and open field (medieval)
scenes.dramatic_function: Mythic set piece: past-life-feeling first kiss with Sionna; vulnerability and surrender.
scenes.summary: Sun through trees; Character_P rides close behind Sionna on a brown mare through lush forest (moss, trillium, bleeding hearts); rose-and-warm-grass scent of her neck; open field; he in white ballooning sleeves and red embroidered tunic helps her down; electric embrace; first kiss; lightest blue eyes; drowning in love.
scenes.continuity_notes: Period wardrobe only; Sionna long brown hair to small of back in dream (allowed dream variance); same face/eyes identity lock.
spoiler_constraints: No given name for Character_P.
Present as dream experience—not confirmed past life yet.

music_intent.mood_arc: pastoral wonder → intimacy → overwhelming first kiss

source_excerpts.quote: The sun is shining through the trees. Millions of specks of sunlight shine down on me and around me.
I am sitting very close behind her, holding on around her waist. Occasionally, I lean forward to place my face next to her neck… mix of rose and warm grass.
I reach for her, and lift her gently as she drops down from the trusty dappled mare… I kiss her, and somehow I know this is the first kiss we have shared.
I look into her eyes, and they are the lightest blue, like the early morning light.

story_beats.intent: Establish dream forest: sun specks, lush foliage, mare.
story_beats.visual_event: Specks of sunlight through thick deciduous canopy; mossy oak/maple; woodland plants; brown/dappled mare moving softly over wood sorrel.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: The sun is shining through the trees. Millions of specks of sunlight. A deciduous forest from long ago.

story_beats.intent: Riding behind her; scent of neck.
story_beats.visual_event: Character_P sits close behind Character_Sionna on the mare, arms around her waist; long brown hair; he leans to her neck, eyes close at rose-and-warm-grass scent.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Not as intoxicating as the smell of her neck—rose and warm grass.
story_beats.blocking_notes: He behind her on saddle; intimate but tender.

story_beats.intent: Emerge into open field; insects on grass blades.
story_beats.visual_event: They ride from forest into uninterrupted sunny field; long drooping grass; small insects alight and buzz; pause center field.
story_beats.speaker: none

story_beats.intent: He dismounts in period clothes; helps her down.
story_beats.visual_event: He jumps down—white ballooning linen sleeves, embroidered red tunic, brown hose—arms outstretched; lifts her gently from the mare; fair heart-shaped face close.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: With arms outstretched I notice the fine linen of my white sleeves… an embroidered red tunic. I lift her gently from the mare.

story_beats.intent: Electric embrace; first kiss; surrender; lightest blue eyes.
story_beats.visual_event: Palpable pull; they embrace and kiss—first kiss energy; he surrenders; ECU her early-morning light blue eyes.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: The charge draws us together. I kiss her—the first kiss we have shared. Finest hour of my life. I surrender completely. I am drowning in her. Eyes like the early morning light.

scenes.setting: Night — Character_P’s cold bedroom
scenes.dramatic_function: Snap back: dream residue; is it book bleed or something real?
scenes.summary: He bolts upright screaming “Hold!”; sweating in cold room; heart pounding; remembers forest, horse, kiss, old clothes; knew the girl—Sionna; questions if he inserted life into Wuthering Heights make-believe.
scenes.continuity_notes: Cold room vs sweat; immediate cut from kiss.
spoiler_constraints: No given name for Character_P.
Do not confirm past life as fact—keep his doubt.

music_intent.mood_arc: shock → disorientation → uneasy wonder

source_excerpts.quote: “Hold!” I screamed, as I bolted upright in bed. My stomach was tight, my chest pounding like a drum. I was sweating, even though it was cold in my room.
It was Sionna, but in a different place with old clothes on. What is happening? Did I insert these people from life into some kind of Wuthering Heights book?

story_beats.intent: Bolt awake: Hold!
story_beats.visual_event: Hard cut to Character_P bolting upright in bed, screaming; sweat; cold room; hand on chest.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Hold!

story_beats.intent: Body panic; what the hell was that.
story_beats.visual_event: He sits breathing hard; looks around ordinary bedroom; residual dream in eyes.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: What the hell was that? I hardly ever dreamed anything. I remember it all—girl on the horse, forest, field, kiss. Clothes like a movie about old kings. It felt like my life for real.

story_beats.intent: Recognize Sionna; doubt make-believe.
story_beats.visual_event: He stares into dark; unsettled half-smile/fear; lies back slowly without peace.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: I knew that girl! Sionna—different place, old clothes. Did I insert people from life into Wuthering Heights? Make-believe. It had to be. Didn’t it?

scenes.setting: Day — Java Hut / talk with Sionna (later week)
scenes.dramatic_function: Philosophy of beauty and dreams; she legitimizes his inner life; he feels he matters.
scenes.summary: VO on beauty not skin deep—Sionna beautiful inside and out; he told her the crazy dream; she smiles through him; Buddhists and dream-as-reality; chair once tree; past-life suggestion; he wishes to return; she makes him feel not just pizza guy/little brother.
scenes.continuity_notes: Story day later same week; regular meeting energy; he has told her the dream.
spoiler_constraints: No given name for Character_P.
Past life remains her suggestion / his hope—not proven.

music_intent.mood_arc: essay warmth → mind-bend → belonging

source_excerpts.quote: I have started to find that some people are beautiful inside as well as out. Sionna is one of those people.
I told her about my crazy dream. She just smiled and looked through me with those watery eyes.
“Did you know that Buddhists consider everything around us to be a dream?” she said.
“Maybe you were dreaming about something from a past life” she said.
She made me feel like I belonged somewhere; like I really mattered.

story_beats.intent: VO essay: beauty not only skin deep; Sionna is a 10 inside.
story_beats.visual_event: Java Hut table again; Sionna listening; his soft gaze.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Somebody said beauty is only skin deep—that’s not true. Sionna is beautiful inside as well as out. I could talk to her all day.

story_beats.intent: He told her the dream; she doesn’t think it’s crazy.
story_beats.visual_event: He talks; she smiles and looks through him with watery eyes.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: I told her about my crazy dream. She just smiled and looked through me. She didn’t think it was crazy.
story_beats.blocking_notes: Her eyeline steady into him—uncanny calm.

story_beats.intent: Buddhists: everything a dream; chair cycle.
story_beats.visual_event: She explains; he frowns then listens; hand on chair.
story_beats.speaker: Character_Sionna
story_beats.dialogue: Did you know that Buddhists consider everything around us to be a dream? It’s not fake—it just won’t last more than the present moment. Once this was a tree, now it’s a chair, someday it returns to earth. We have to be willing to let go—and accept—without grasping.

story_beats.intent: His pushback then openness.
story_beats.visual_event: He gestures at chair, air, her; then softens.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: I don’t get that. I’m sitting in a chair, breathing air, looking at you. How could all this be just fake?

story_beats.intent: Past-life suggestion; he hopes she was the girl.
story_beats.visual_event: She leans in slightly; he lights with hope.
story_beats.speaker: Character_Sionna
story_beats.dialogue: Maybe you were dreaming about something from a past life.

story_beats.intent: Belonging VO: not just pizza guy or pesky little brother.
story_beats.visual_event: Café noise softens subjectively; he looks at her with gratitude.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: If that was a past life, I wish I could go back—especially the girl. She made me feel like I belonged. Not just a guy who makes pizzas, or takes care of Ma, or somebody’s pesky little brother. For once I felt important.

scenes.setting: Day — public library stacks and reading table
scenes.dramatic_function: Habit forms: he reads to keep up with her; Freud and subconscious desire.
scenes.summary: After talks he goes to library to not feel dumb; starts liking reading; finds Freud—ego, dream analysis, everything sexual; concludes subconscious wants horse, olden times, fall in love.
scenes.continuity_notes: Recurring secret library habit; books on psychology/dreams.
spoiler_constraints: No given name for Character_P on any materials.

music_intent.mood_arc: diligence → wry realization

source_excerpts.quote: When we talked about something, I would go to the library and try to read about it, so I wouldn’t feel so dumb.
Talking about dreams made me read about Sigmund Freud. … He came up with the ego, and dream analysis.
If that’s true, then in my subconscious I want to ride a horse, live in olden times, and fall in love with a girl. Sounds good to me.

story_beats.intent: Library return: reading to match Sionna.
story_beats.visual_event: Character_P at library table with stack; focused, not lost like first night.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: When we talked about something, I’d go to the library so I wouldn’t feel so dumb. The thing is, I started to really like reading.

story_beats.intent: Freud books; sexual subconscious theory.
story_beats.visual_event: Inserts of psychology book pages/spines; he raises eyebrows at sexual emphasis.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Dreams made me read Sigmund Freud—German guy, psychotherapy, ego, dream analysis. Seems like everything he talked about was sexual. Dreams tell what’s in the subconscious.

story_beats.intent: Wry acceptance of his subconscious wish list.
story_beats.visual_event: He closes book with a small private smile.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: If that’s true, then in my subconscious I want to ride a horse, live in olden times, and fall in love with a girl. Sounds good to me.

scenes.setting: Various days — park, museum, yoga studio
scenes.dramatic_function: Relationship expands into culture and body; yoga as strangest step yet—chunk ends mid-initiation.
scenes.summary: Regular meetings: Java Hut talks, park walks, museums (crazy painters; intelligence borders insanity); he never told anyone the weirdest thing—yoga on a purple mat among ~20 people, eyes closed on breath, feeling stupid in t-shirt and shorts as class moves into upside-down/leg-spread poses.
scenes.continuity_notes: Montage compresses regular dating; yoga is first class; chunk text cuts mid-yoga description—end on his discomfort in poses, not a finished class arc.
spoiler_constraints: No given name for Character_P.
No public announcement of relationship to Nick/Ma yet.

music_intent.style: light montage then sparse yoga drone
music_intent.mood_arc: expanding world → museum wryness → vulnerable absurdity on the mat

source_excerpts.quote: Sionna and I continued to meet on a regular basis. Most of the time, we just sat around and talked at the Java Hut, or walked in the park, or went to museums.
Sionna said something like, "intelligence borders insanity" or something like that.
I found myself lying on a purple rectangle mat closing my eyes and thinking about breathing. I felt like the stupidest person on earth.
I was wearing a t-shirt, and shorts, which, as it turns out, is not exactly the thing to wear to yoga.

story_beats.intent: Montage: regular meetings—talks, park, museums.
story_beats.visual_event: Quick beats: walking park path with Sionna; museum gallery staring at intense paintings; shared quiet smiles.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: We kept meeting—Java Hut, park walks, museums. I hadn’t spent much time in museums as a kid. Got pretty interesting.

story_beats.intent: Painters crazy; intelligence borders insanity.
story_beats.visual_event: They stand before a dramatic painting; she comments; he smirks.
story_beats.speaker: Character_Sionna
story_beats.dialogue: Intelligence borders insanity.

story_beats.intent: His wry VO: never knew anyone smart enough to be insane—until this path.
story_beats.visual_event: They exit museum into day; he half-laughs to himself.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: I think I’ve never known anyone smart enough to be considered insane—just insane people who were also dumb. Which leads me to the weirdest thing I’ve ever done.

story_beats.intent: Yoga room: purple mat, eyes closed, twenty others; feels stupid.
story_beats.visual_event: Yoga studio; ~20 people on purple rectangle mats; Character_P in t-shirt and shorts lies eyes closed trying to think about breathing; Sionna nearby more at ease.
story_beats.shot_scale_hint: wide studio to close on him
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: I found myself on a purple mat closing my eyes thinking about breathing. Felt like the stupidest person on earth. Funny thing—about twenty other people doing the same thing.

story_beats.intent: She sold benefits; wrong clothes; class moves into hard poses—end mid-discomfort.
story_beats.visual_event: Class shifts: leaning upside down, legs spreading; his t-shirt rides up, shorts awkward; face: what am I doing—hold end on his strained pose among calmer students.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: I don’t know how she talked me into this. Centuries old—calm the mind, lung capacity, stay young forever. Sounded like a miracle drug. T-shirt and shorts—not exactly the thing. A lot of leaning upside down, spreading your legs apart—
story_beats.blocking_notes: Sionna in proper yoga wear nearby; he mirrors poorly; no dialogue needed from instructor.
